intro_library_01: ^It's midnight in mid December in Montreal.
intro_library_01_1: ^Snow flecks the Vanier library windows, and the lights hum the way they do when most people have gone home.
intro_library_01_2: ^The library is open for all-night study sessions during exams.
intro_library_02: ^You and Malik have been here for hours, trying to finish a project that still feels too big.
intro_library_02_b: ^You missed too many deadlines and barely passed.
intro_library_02_1: ^You tried to get organized, but things kept sliding.
intro_library_02_3: ^You promised this semester would be different.
intro_library_03_2: ^You check the time without wanting to.
intro_library_03_3: ^The minute hand keeps moving either way.
intro_cold_flicker_01: ^The library changes in ways that are hard to name.
intro_cold_flicker_01_1a: ^The heat seeps away from the room.
intro_cold_flicker_01_2: ^Fluorescents flicker once, then settle into a sickly yellow.
intro_cold_flicker_02: ^A draft moves through the stacks, colder than it should be.
intro_cold_flicker_02_1: ^It smells like candle smoke and old wax.
intro_cold_flicker_02_2: ^Somewhere in the dark, a book falls—loud enough to make both of you flinch.
intro_cold_flicker_03: ^Malik (low): People say this place gets weird after midnight.
intro_cold_flicker_04: ^You slide your agenda into your bag.
intro_agnes_appears1: ^A figure stands at the end of the aisle, half in shadow.
intro_agnes_appears1_1: ^The fabric of her habit hangs in thin folds.
intro_agnes_appears1_2: ^Where her face should be warm, there is only cool darkness.
intro_agnes_appears2: ^Sister Agnes: When Vanier was a convent I taught here.
intro_agnes_appears2_1: ^You remind me of two of my own pupils.
intro_agnes_appears2_3: ^They never finished their thesis because I missed too much.
intro_agnes_appears2_5: ^The thesis was lost, and Vanier has kept me and my students here for all these years.
intro_agnes_appears3: ^Sister Agnes: Now Vanier has its eyes on you.
intro_agnes_appears3_1: ^You must complete the lost thesis before dawn.
intro_agnes_appears3_2: ^Find the missing sources and finish it.
intro_agnes_appears3_4: ^Sister Agnes: One page lies in the tunnels under the N building.
intro_agnes_appears3_6: ^Bring them to the archives and complete the thesis.
intro_agnes_appears3_8: ^We need to get out of here!
intro_agnes_appears3_choice: ^Do you believe what you just saw?
d1_setup_01: ^Sister Agnes thins like smoke and parts on a draft.
d1_setup_01_1: ^The scent of old paper clings to your sleeves.
d1_setup_01_3: ^A new icon wakes on the screen, red and steady.
d1_setup_01_4: ^Malik's hands shake and the phone slips, but you catch it before it hits the floor.
d1_setup_02: ^The DEADline App has installed itself on your phone.
d1_setup_02_1: ^Before dawn, find two missing working pages and finish your part.
^In the tunnels: Find the evening study schedule.
^At the cemetery: Find the intentions log.
d1_setup_02_2: ^How do you want to keep track of everything?
^Set up a single folder in your cloud drive (+3m)
^Make a paper list in your notebook (+2m)
d1_setup_04: ^The DEADline app suggests some things you might want to have tonight.
^Do you want to build a small document care kit (microfiber cloth, clear sleeves, binder clips)?
after_kit: ^Do you want to set up a quick way to take clear photos of any page you find?
d1_choice: ^You check the next steps in the DEADline App.
d1_choice_1: ^Before dawn, find the missing pages and finish the thesis.
d1_research_01_1: ^The room is colder than it should be.
d1_research_02_1: ^The library was once a convent chapel.
d1_research_02_2: ^Two cemeteries sit beside the grounds, one for the parish, one for the Sisters.
d1_research_02_3: ^In the 1960s, records were moved in a rush when the site changed hands.
d1_research_02_4: ^You find conflicting notes about chalk marks in the tunnels.
d1_research_02_5: ^Some say they mark safe routes; others say they fade.
t_setup_01_1a: ^The air from below smells like damp brick and old heating.
t_setup_01_3: ^Your phone light makes a small circle that looks smaller the longer you stare at it.
t_setup_01_5: ^Your phone shows a small note: *Find the study schedule page.*
t_setup_01_6: ^A small card opens on the screen:
^Set a quick route plan: mark turns, map as you go, check in with Malik every 5 minutes (+3m)
^Move by instinct. Adjust as you go (+2m)

c-0: ^• Objective: Find the study schedule page used to plan a night of work.
^• Clue: Follow service lines toward maintenance rooms; match chalk ghosts at junctions.
^• Why it matters: Gives you language you can cite and a plan you can use tonight.

b: ^Use your earlier research: match chalk photos to wall marks (+2m)

t_setup_04_a: ^Your steps echo into places you can't see.

b: ^You set a workable rhythm. Walk five minutes, then stop, mark, check...
^You move with the draft and the sound of water...
^You hold the chalk marks in your head or in your photos...

t_setup_04_1: ^Hug the service line; work each branch in order (adds
^Cut a faster diagonal; stop at each junction to check marks (adds
^At each turn, leave a sign and verify chalk vs photo before committing (adds
t_find: ^The corridor kinks left, then right, and opens into a low space that smells like oil and mop water.
t_find_1: ^The pipe widens and the floor slopes to a drain.
t_find_1a: ^It is a maintenance room, like you expected.
t_find_2: ^On the back of a service door, under a film of dust, something waits where no one thinks to tidy up.
t_find_3a: ^The handwriting is steady even where the paper buckles.

b: ^Your detour costs a few minutes, but you made it.

t_find_buzz: ^Your DEADline App buzzes in your pocket.
t_page_principles: ^The DEADline App shows a new achievement called *Principle Scroll*:
^1) Plan and track: keep one trusted list of tasks and due dates. Break work into steps. Finish on time.
^2) Timeline and buffer: give each step a slice of time and a little slack so delays don't break the plan.
^3) Mark and verify: leave yourself signs and check them so you don't lose your place.
t_page_principles_1: ^The app marks the tunnels task complete.
c_entry_01_1: ^The dates on the nearest stones sit like held breaths.
c_entry_01_2: ^Your phone hums, then shows a small note before going still.
c_entry_01_3: ^The note reads: *"Leave with the page intact."*
c_entry_01_4: ^Malik (hushed): Okay. What are we trying to leave with?
c_entry_01_5: ^A small card opens on the screen:

c-0: ^• Objective: Recover the intentions page intact.
^• Clue: Look near a gate box: drier edge, faint rust shadow, disturbed gravel.
^• Why it matters: Goals that hold — clear promises with times you can check.

c_setup_01_2: ^You steady your notebook on a dry stone.
c_setup_01_3: ^The phone's note lingers at the edge of your thoughts.
c_setup_01_4: ^You keep thinking: *"Leave with the page intact."* Not just *found.* Intact.
c_setup_01_5: ^Sister Agnes (near): Write exactly what you will finish, and by when.
c_setup_01_choices: ^Quick aim: find the page fast and protect it later (+1m)
^Co-write the goals with Malik: you scan, he marks turns (+3m)
c_search: ^Your words pull the night into shape.

b: ^You move along the higher ground where the grass is paler and the stones stay clean longer.
^You cut diagonals through the tombstones, counting under your breath.
^Malik taps your shoulder at quiet intervals and touches the fence at each turn so you don't loop.
^Ten minutes in, you've covered two rows and marked three to check again.
^When you point at a likely spot, he nods and draws a tiny X on the page.
^At forty, you circle back to a disturbance you missed the first time.
^You tell yourself you'll sort the rest out later.

c_search_3: ^Work the edge and take it row by row (adds
^Cut a fast grid and chase fresh signs (adds
^Mark your path and verify before you commit (adds
c_find: ^Behind a cracked stone, half a handspan above the wet line, you spot it:
^a shallow hollow with a rust lip where something's been and gone and come back.
c_find_1: ^The tin is there, cold and ordinary.
c_find_2: ^Inside, a single page; the paper thin as breath, the ink still dark.
c_find_3: ^You said you'd leave with it intact. What do you do?

b: ^The text lies flat. Your shoulders come down when you check the time and see you're still on schedule.
^The fragile page rips. You can still read it, but you'll have to handle it carefully now.
^ minutes getting the page home safe.
^You seal it in the sleeve, note the gate box in your notebook,
^and walk the rows back the way you came without cutting corners.

c_page_principles: ^At the gate, your phone buzzes and a new achievement appears on the DEADline App.
c_page_principles_1: ^– Specific & Relevant: say exactly what "done" is and why.
^– Measurable & Time-bound: put a time to it so you know.
^– Achievable with a plan: choose a route and guardrails that fit the place.
a_entry_01: ^The archive door drags shut and seals the air.
a_entry_01_1: ^A desk lamp breathes a small circle of light.
a_entry_01_2: ^Beyond it, the shelves lean like ribs.
a_entry_01_3: ^The typewriter rattles a few keys on its own and stops, as if it choked on a word.
a_entry_01_4: ^Your phone shows one line and a clock that does not blink: *"Finish and hand it in."*
a_entry_01_5: ^A small card opens on the screen:

c-0: ^• Objective: Build a clean section from the two pages, then use them to finish the thesis.
^• Clue: Make headings from the page lines, read aloud, ask for help, cut once more.
^• Why it matters: Finished, readable work is what will let you leave this place.

a_entry_01_8: ^Sister Agnes (near): Finish before dawn, or walk these halls with me.
a_setup_01: ^You place the study schedule page in the light.
a_setup_01_1: ^The principles you found in the tunnels sit at the top of your notes, waiting to breathe.
a_setup_01_2: ^Sister Agnes (near): Stop collecting scraps and build the page you will hand in.
a_setup_01_choices: ^What approach do you take to finish?

b: ^You don't have enough time for any proper finishing method.
^Do what you can (run out of time)

a_result: ^You sit back and listen to the building breathe.

b:
^The draft reads in one breath. Your file names make sense even to future-you.

^The schedule, intentions, and revision lines sit inside your work now, visible in the steps.

^When you flip a page, the typewriter doesn't flinch.

^It exists, whole enough to submit, but the seams show.

^Two headings almost rhyme by accident. A draft paragraph you meant to delete is probably still in there.

^The lamp hums like it has an opinion.
^It reads like it knew what you would ask before you asked it.
^Malik taps the closing line and grins his quick, thin grin — proud but trying not to show it.

a_result_2: ^Sister Agnes (near): A finished section carries you farther than a pile of notes.
a_page_principles: ^Your phone buzzes and the final achievement appears on the DEADline App.
a_page_principles_1: ^• Integrate and check: gather the pieces, shape one clean version, then verify it reads the way you intend.
^• Use feedback: ask a reader what is unclear or missing and act on what you hear.
^• Revise and improve: small, focused edits lift the work; take initiative to close gaps.

b: ^Sister Agnes gathers from the corners of the room as dawn bleaches the windows.
^Sister Agnes: This is the Temporal Organizer. It belongs to those who finish what they start and care for their work as if it were living.
^Your phone changes in your hand. It keeps clean versions, remembers next steps, and does the small things that used to trip you.
^Malik: It feels like the opposite of panic. With this, we're going to be able to finish our own project and get through the rest of the semester.
^Walk out into the snow with Malik
^Light pushes at the windows and then hesitates. Sister Agnes stands near the typewriter, not quite smiling.
^Sister Agnes: You escaped the worst of it. Keep one list you trust. Leave room to adjust. Ask for help when you can't see what's missing.
^Malik: That was close... and weird. We'll have to do better next time.
^The library swallows the light as the clock nears
^The carriage slides home with a sound like a lid.
^Sister Agnes: This place keeps what's unfinished. Stay. Help me keep the shelves in order.
^The halls do not let you go

end_epilogue_success: ^Cold air bites your cheeks as the doors open.
end_epilogue_success_1: ^Snow drifts down in small, forgiving flakes.
end_epilogue_success_2: ^Your phone shows *Today, Next Up,* and a tidy list that matches what you actually do.
end_epilogue_success_4: ^But I know what to do next. And after that.
end_epilogue_success_5: ^In the glass of the foyer, for a heartbeat, Sister Agnes nods once and thins to daylight.
end_epilogue_mixed: ^Later that day, your reflection looks steadier.
end_epilogue_mixed_1: ^You missed some polish, but your plan kept you on time.
end_epilogue_mixed_2: ^You write a short check-in for yourself and add one change you will try next week.
end_epilogue_failure_2: ^Footsteps that are not yours pass by and do not pause.
end_epilogue_failure_4: ^You feel him count his breaths like you did in the stairwell.
end_epilogue_failure_5: ^Sister Agnes: There is work to do.
end_epilogue_failure_6: ^We will teach the living how to finish.
task_hub: ^The red icon pulses once, then settles. Your tasks sit in a neat column:

b: ^• Tunnels: Study Schedule Page — Not yet found
^• Cemetery: Intentions Log — Not yet found
^A faint outline of the Archives task brightens as if waiting for you.
^The Archives task remains dim, locked until the others are complete.

task_hub: ^Check your notes and pause a moment

c-0: ^You breathe in the paper-and-dust air. The building waits.
